title: CBIC Revises Tariff Values for Imported Gold, Silver & Edible Oils
content:
# CBIC Revises Tariff Values for Imported Gold, Silver & Edible Oils

Ek important update hai! The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) ne several imported commodities ke liye tariff values revise ki hain. This move aims to manage import costs amidst global commodity price fluctuations.

Here are the key details:

*   **Affected Commodities:** The revised tariff values apply to crude palm oil, RBD palm oil, crude palmolein, RBD palmolein, crude soybean oil, brass scrap, gold, silver, and areca nuts.
*   **Notification Details:** This revision was announced through Notification No. 46/2026-Customs (N.T.) dated May 15, 2026, and became effective from May 16, 2026.
*   **Updated Tariffs:**
    *   For Gold: US$ 1508 per 10 grams.
    *   For Silver: US$ 2810 per kilogram.
*   **Reason for Adjustment:** This adjustment reflects the government's efforts to manage import costs in light of fluctuating global commodity prices.

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.gst.gov.in/newsandupdates/readmore/1013401)

*Published on: 18 May 2026*
